---
title: "FRET_assay.md"
output: html_document
date: "2026-02-21"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

# Install packages 
```{r}
install.packages('optparse')
install.packages('readxl')
install.packages('dplyr')
install.packages('tidyr')
install.packages('stringr')
install.packages('lubridate')
install.packages('purrr')
install.packages('readr')
install.packages('writexl')
install.packages('stats')
install.packages('ggplot2')
install.packages('forcats')
```


# Modules
```{r}
source("parse_plate.R")
source("core_analysis_v2.R")
source("activity_analysis.R")
```

# Data cleaning
```{r}
# Name of the raw Excel file exported from the plate reader
input_file <- "SUBGVP_Esin_20feb26.xlsx"

# Which sheet in that Excel file to use.
# Can be sheet index (1, 2, 3, ...) or a sheet name like "Results"
sheet_id <- 1

# Row range for the DONOR block (inclusive)
# This should start at the header row for the donor block
donor_start_row <- 34
donor_end_row   <- 684

# Row range for the ACCEPTOR block (inclusive)
# This should start at the header row for the acceptor block
acceptor_start_row <- 687
acceptor_end_row   <- 1387

# Name for the tidy CSV output you want to generate
output_file <- "cleaned_data_FRET_20feb2026.csv"


## 4. Run the processing
tidy_data <- process_plate_file(
  input           = input_file,
  sheet           = sheet_id,
  donor_start     = donor_start_row,
  donor_end       = donor_end_row,
  acceptor_start  = acceptor_start_row,
  acceptor_end    = acceptor_end_row,
  output          = output_file
)


## 5. Inspect a preview
cat("\n=== Preview of processed data ===\n")
print(tail(tidy_data, n = 10))

cat("\nColumns in output:\n")
print(colnames(tidy_data))

cat("\nOutput written to: ", output_file, "\n")


```

```{r}
# 2. User parameters (EDIT THESE)
tidy_csv_file        <- "cleaned_data_FRET_20feb2026.csv"
plate_map_file       <- "SUBGVP_Esin_20feb26_platemap.csv"
qc_output_file       <- "SUBGVP_Esin_core_output_QC.xlsx"
rds_output_file      <- "SUBGVP_core_results.Rds"

S0_uM                <- 5          # starting substrate concentration (µM)
fit_window_min       <- 5          # legacy only; tracked in refs
fraction_ceiling     <- 0.30       # fraction cleaved cutoff for slope fit
wt_variant           <- "SubG"     # reference variant
wt_wells_for_Rmax    <- NULL       # NULL = use all wells per variant for plateau detection

plateau_points       <- 3          # fallback tail averaging window if no plateau detected
plateau_span_cycles  <- 15         # how many cycles forward to check plateau stability
plateau_tolerance    <- 0.01       # <=1% drift over that window => plateau

# 3. Run pipeline
results_list <- run_core_pipeline_from_tidy(
  tidy_csv_file        = tidy_csv_file,
  plate_map_file       = plate_map_file,
  S0_uM                = S0_uM,
  fit_window_min       = fit_window_min,
  fraction_ceiling     = fraction_ceiling,
  wt_wells_for_Rmax    = wt_wells_for_Rmax,
  plateau_points       = plateau_points,
  plateau_span_cycles  = plateau_span_cycles,
  plateau_tolerance    = plateau_tolerance,
  qc_output_file       = qc_output_file
)



# 5. Save full results to RDS for reuse
saveRDS(results_list, rds_output_file)

# 6. Quick console summary
cat("\n=== Core pipeline finished ===\n")
cat("Input tidy data:           ", tidy_csv_file,  "\n")
cat("Input plate map:           ", plate_map_file, "\n")
cat("QC workbook written to:    ", qc_output_file, "\n")
cat("Full result list saved to: ", rds_output_file, "\n\n")
cat("Reference values / settings:\n")
print(results_list$refs)
```
